title: 10 Soothing Herbal Teas for Oily Face and Acne Prone Skin
intro_desc: Drinking herbal teas can be beneficial for people with oily skin because it helps to balance the body's natural oil production. Some herbs, like 'Rosmarinus officinalis' (rosemary), are known for their ability to improve circulation and reduce stress, which can contribute to oily skin. When we're stressed, our body produces more oil to protect the skin, leading to an oily complexion. Rosemary tea can help to calm the body and mind, reducing the production of excess oil.  Another herb that can help with oily skin is 'Lavandula angustifolia' (lavender). Lavender tea has natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of pores and oiliness. Lavender also has a calming effect on the mind and body, which can help to reduce stress and promote a healthy balance of oil production.  It's essential to address oily skin issues because it can lead to acne, clogged pores, and other skin problems. Oily skin can also make you feel self-conscious and affect your confidence. Drinking herbal teas like rosemary and lavender can help to improve your skin health and overall well-being. By drinking these teas, you can reduce stress, improve your circulation, and promote a healthy balance of oil production, leading to a clearer and more radiant complexion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis tea, also known as rosemary tea, is a great choice for those with oily skin. This is because rosemary has natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling on the skin. The antioxidants present in rosemary tea also help to combat free radicals that can cause skin problems and lead to oily skin. When consumed as a tea, rosemary can help to improve blood circulation, which promotes healthy skin and reduces the appearance of pores. Additionally, rosemary tea has been shown to have a balancing effect on the body's natural oil production, helping to regulate the amount of oil produced on the skin. Drinking rosemary tea can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to oily skin. By drinking rosemary tea regularly, you may notice a reduction in the appearance of oily skin and an improvement in your overall skin health.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for oily face because it has strong antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent clogged pores, thus controlling the production of excess oil.

Carnosic acid is good for an oily face because it has antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and prevent the overproduction of sebum, which can clog pores and lead to an oily complexion.
Polyphenols are good for oily face because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce acne and prevent clogged pores, making skin look clearer and more balanced.
Flavonoids are good for oily face because they have antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and prevent the production of sebum, which is the oil that causes an oily face.
Tannins have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce oil production in the skin, making it a good natural remedy for controlling oily faces.

remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for oily face
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal tea that helps with an oily face, you'll need some dried Silybum marianum leaves and flowers, a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup, boiling water, and a strainer. First, boil a cup of water and then pour it over 1-2 teaspoons of dried Silybum marianum leaves and flowers in the tea infuser or directly into the cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has cooled down to a comfortable temperature. After that, strain the liquid through a strainer to remove the herbs. Once you have your tea, you can drink it as is or add a bit of honey to taste. Drinking 1-2 cups of this tea per day may help reduce the oil production in your skin, leading to a clearer and less oily face. However, please note that individual results may vary and it's always a good idea to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tea is a great choice for people with oily skin. This is because it has natural properties that help to balance the skin's oil production. The tea contains antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and calm the skin, which can help to prevent clogged pores and breakouts. The antioxidants also help to repair and protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oily skin. Additionally, the tea has a cooling effect that can help to reduce redness and soothe the skin, making it less likely to produce excess oil. Furthermore, the tea's antimicrobial properties can help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause acne and other skin problems. By drinking Lavandula angustifolia tea, you can help to regulate your skin's oil production and achieve a healthier, more balanced complexion.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for an oily face because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and soothe irritated skin, while also possessing antimicrobial properties that can help control the growth of bacteria that can clog pores and cause acne.

Linalyl acetate in Silybum marianate is good for an oily face because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and calm the skin, while also possessing antibacterial properties that prevent the growth of bacteria that can clog pores and cause more oil production.
Apigenin is good for oily face because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce sebum production and prevent pores from clogging.
Tannins are good for oily face because they help to reduce sebum production and prevent bacteria growth that can clog pores, leading to a more balanced and healthier skin.

remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for oily face
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal tea that helps with an oily face, you will need some dried Silybum marianum leaves or flowers. Start by boiling one liter of water in a pot. Once the water is boiling, add two to three teaspoons of dried Silybum marianum leaves or flowers. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let it brew for 5-7 minutes. After the brewing time is up, remove the pot from the heat and let it steep for another 2-3 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the Silybum marianum leaves or flowers. You can add honey or lemon to taste, if you like. Let the tea cool down before drinking it. It's recommended to drink the tea two to three times a day to see the best results in reducing oily skin. However, you should consult a doctor or a healthcare professional before using Silybum marianum herbal tea, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tea, also known as lemon balm tea, is a great option for people with oily skin. The main reason is that it has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and irritation on the skin. The tea also contains antioxidants that protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. This can lead to a reduction in the production of sebum, the oily substance produced by the skin, thereby helping to control oiliness. Additionally, the tea has a calming effect, which can soothe and balance the skin's natural pH levels. This balance can help to reduce oil production and prevent clogged pores, making it easier to manage oily skin.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for oily face because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and soothe the skin, also its antioxidant properties help to reduce the production of sebum which can cause oily skin.
Luteolin is good for oily face because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce sebum production and prevent clogged pores.
Apigenin is good for oily face because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and soothe skin, thereby controlling oil production and improving skin balance.

remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal teas for oily face
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal tea for an oily face, you will need some dried or fresh Silybum marianum leaves and flowers. First, boil a cup of water and let it cool down for a minute or two. Then, add 1-2 teaspoons of the dried leaves and flowers to the water, or use 3-4 fresh ones. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, so the active ingredients can release into the water. After steeping, strain the tea using a piece of cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the leaves and flowers. Once the tea is strained, let it cool down to a comfortable temperature. You can then use it as a face wash by dipping a cotton ball or pad into the tea and applying it to your face, or drink it as a tea to help detoxify your body and reduce oil production. It is recommended to use this tea 1-2 times a day for best results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tea is a great remedy for oily skin. It helps to balance the skin's natural oil production, reducing the appearance of pores and making the skin look smoother. The antioxidants in aloe vera tea help to fight off bacteria and other impurities that can clog pores and cause acne. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation. The tea's natural astringent properties help to tighten the skin, which can help to minimize the appearance of large pores and fine lines. Drinking aloe vera tea can also help to detoxify the body, removing toxins that can cause skin problems from the inside out. Regular consumption of aloe vera tea can lead to a decrease in oil production, a reduction in acne, and a more even skin tone, resulting in a clearer and healthier complexion.

Polyphenols, also known as Milk Thistle, are good for oily faces because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and inflammation caused by excess oil, and also have antioxidant properties that help to protect the skin from damage and promote a healthy balance of oil production.

remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal teas for oily face
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal tea for an oily face, start by gathering dried Silybum marianum leaves and flowers. You can find these in health food stores or online. Begin by boiling a cup of water and then adding a teaspoon of the dried Silybum marianum herbs. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the active ingredients to release into the water. Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids. Once the tea has cooled, you can apply it to your face as a toner or use it as a face mask by soaking a cotton pad in the tea and applying it to your skin for 10-15 minutes. After rinsing the tea off your face, pat dry with a towel and follow up with your regular skincare routine. Drinking the Silybum marianum tea can also help reduce oil production from the inside out, but be sure to dilute it with water first as it can be quite strong.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus tea, also known as lemongrass tea, is a natural remedy that can help with oily skin. The tea has antiseptic and antibacterial properties that help to reduce the production of oil in the skin. This is because lemongrass contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have been shown to inhibit the activity of sebum-producing glands in the skin. When these glands produce less oil, the skin appears less oily and pores are smaller, giving a cleaner and smoother complexion. Additionally, lemongrass tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and irritation associated with oily skin, making it a soothing and calming drink for those with acne-prone skin. Drinking lemongrass tea regularly may also help to balance the skin's natural pH, reducing the risk of clogged pores and breakouts.

remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal teas for oily face
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal tea for an oily face, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need a handful of Silybum marianum leaves, which you can purchase from a health food store or grow yourself. Rinse the leaves thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or impurities. Next, combine the leaves with one liter of boiling water in a large pot, making sure the leaves are fully submerged. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced slightly and the leaves have lost their potency. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the leaves. Let the tea cool to room temperature before storing it in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. To use the tea, simply dip a cotton ball into the cooled liquid and apply it to your face, letting it sit for 10-15 minutes before rinsing with warm water. Repeat this process daily for best results in reducing oily skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tea, also known as thyme tea, is a natural remedy for oily skin. The antioxidants and antibacterial properties in thyme help to reduce the amount of oil produced by the skin. This is because thyme tea contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause acne. When you drink thyme tea, these compounds are absorbed into your bloodstream and then travel to your skin, where they help to balance the production of oil. Thyme tea also helps to unclog pores, which can become blocked with oil and bacteria, leading to acne and other skin problems. As a result, drinking thyme tea may help to reduce the appearance of oily skin and prevent breakouts.

Polyphenols, also known as milk thistle, are good for an oily face because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling, and also have antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to clogged pores and oil buildup.

remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal teas for oily face
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal tea for an oily face, you will first need to dry the Silybum marianum flowers and seeds. This can be done by spreading them out on a piece of paper or a clean cloth in a warm, dry place. Once they are completely dry, you can use a mortar and pestle to crush them into a fine powder. Next, you will need to measure out the correct amount of Silybum marianum powder for your tea - a general rule of thumb is to use one teaspoon of the powder per cup of boiling water. Place the powder into a teapot or a heat-resistant cup, and then pour boiling water over it. Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes, or until the liquid has reached your desired strength. Strain the tea into another cup before drinking, and you can add a squeeze of lemon or honey to taste. It's recommended to drink this tea 2-3 times a week, ideally before washing your face, to help reduce the appearance of an oily complexion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tea is a great option for people with oily faces. This is because aloe vera has natural properties that help to balance the skin's pH levels. When your skin's pH levels are out of balance, it can produce more oil, leading to an oily face. Aloe vera tea helps to regulate the skin's pH levels, which can reduce the amount of oil produced. Additionally, aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation. Drinking aloe vera tea can also help to flush out toxins from the body, which can clog pores and cause acne. By reducing oil production and flushing out toxins, aloe vera tea can help to give you a clearer and healthier-looking complexion.

Start by boiling 1 cup of water, then add 1-2 teaspoons of dried Silybum marianum seeds to the water. Reduce the heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes. remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis tea, also known as green tea, is good for an oily face because of its natural ingredients. The tea contains antioxidants called catechins, which help reduce inflammation and fight bacteria that cause acne. When you drink green tea, these antioxidants get absorbed into your bloodstream and help reduce oil production in your skin. This can lead to a decrease in oiliness and a clearer complexion. Additionally, green tea has antiseptic properties that help to kill bacteria that can clog pores and cause breakouts. Green tea also has astringent properties that help to tighten pores and reduce their appearance, which can make your skin look smoother and less oily. Drinking green tea regularly can also help to balance your skin's natural pH, which can help to control oil production and reduce the appearance of oily skin.

When you drink the tea or apply it topically, it can help to reduce redness and irritation that can be caused by oily skin. The tea also has antiseptic properties, which can help to prevent breakouts and acne caused by bacteria. You can buy dried Silybum marianum online or at a local health food store. Boil a cup of water and then add a teaspoon of dried Silybum marianum to the water. Let it steep for 5-10 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced slightly and the flavor has been released. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to use a small amount as the tea can be quite bitter. Drink the tea once or twice a day to help reduce oil production and balance your skin's natural moisture levels. It's also essential to note that Silybum marianum tea may not work for everyone, and you should always consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have sensitive skin or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum tea, also known as milk thistle tea, is a great natural remedy for an oily face. It contains silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and prevent breakouts. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to calm and soothe the skin, reducing redness and irritation. Additionally, silymarin has been shown to improve liver function, which can help to remove toxins and excess oils from the body. This can lead to a reduction in sebum production, resulting in a clearer and more balanced complexion. Furthermore, the tea's antioxidants can also help to protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage, promoting a healthy and glowing complexion. The tea's natural astringent properties can also help to tighten pores, reducing the appearance of large pores and giving the skin a smoother texture.

remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal teas for oily face
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal tea that helps with oily face, start by gathering 1-2 tablespoons of dried milk thistle flowers (Silybum marianum) and 1 cup of boiling water. Pour the boiling water over the dried flowers in a cup and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their medicinal properties. Strain the tea into another cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids. Let the tea cool down, then refrigerate it for later use. When ready to drink, steep 1 teaspoon of the dried flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to make a fresh batch. Drink 1-2 cups of the tea daily, ideally before bedtime, as it is said to help regulate oil production and reduce inflammation in the skin, resulting in a clearer and more balanced complexion.

supplementary_best: To remedy an oily face, a combination of herbal teas made from Calendula officinalis, Thymus vulgaris, and Cymbopogon citratus is recommended. Calendula officinalis is known for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce redness and prevent breakouts. Thymus vulgaris, or thyme, has antimicrobial properties that can help control the growth of bacteria on the skin, reducing oil production and acne. Cymbopogon citratus, or lemongrass, has a balancing effect on the skin, regulating oil production and tightening pores. When brewed together, these teas can create a soothing and purifying effect on the skin, helping to control oiliness and reduce the appearance of acne.
supplementary_avoid: If you have an oily face, you may want to avoid certain herbal teas that can make oil production worse. For example, teas made from dandelion (Taraxacum officinale) can stimulate the liver to produce more bile, which can increase oil production on the skin.  Ginger (Zingiber officinale) tea can also cause blood vessels to dilate, leading to increased oil production. Another herb to be cautious with is saw palmetto (Serenoa repens), which can block the production of hormones that help regulate oil production.  Ginkgo biloba tea can thin the blood and cause blood vessels to dilate, which may increase oil production.  Nettle (Urtica dioica) tea can also cause blood vessels to dilate, and may lead to increased oil production.  Lastly, teas made from burdock (Arctium lappa) can stimulate the liver and increase oil production.
supplementary_other_remedies: Using clay masks can help with an oily face because clay absorbs excess oil and dirt from the skin. When you apply a clay mask, it draws out the impurities, leaving your skin feeling clean and refreshed. This is because clay has a negative charge, which attracts the positive charge of oil and dirt particles, making it easy to remove them from the skin. Clay masks also help to balance the skin's natural pH, which can become imbalanced when the skin produces too much oil.  Another natural remedy that can help with an oily face is using aloe vera gel. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin. It also has soothing properties that can calm and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of pores. Additionally, aloe vera helps to lock in moisture, which can help to reduce the production of oil. This is because when the skin is well-hydrated, it doesn't need to produce as much oil to compensate for dryness.  Exfoliating with sugar or salt can also help with an oily face because it helps to remove dead skin cells and other debris that can clog pores and make the skin look greasy. When you exfoliate, you're helping to unclog pores and allow the skin to breathe, which can help to reduce the production of oil. Exfoliating also helps to improve skin texture, making it look smoother and more radiant.  Using a cold compress can also help to reduce the appearance of oily skin. When you apply a cold compress, it helps to constrict the pores, making it harder for oil to come out. This is because the cold temperature causes the pores to tighten, which can help to reduce the appearance of oil. Additionally, cold compresses can help to reduce redness and inflammation associated with oily skin.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with oily skin often experience other skin problems. For example, they may get acne, which is when pores on the skin get blocked and become inflamed. This is because oily skin has more bacteria, which can cause infections. Medicinal herbs like tea tree oil can help reduce acne by killing bacteria.  Another issue people with oily skin may face is blackheads, which are clogged pores on the skin's surface. Blackheads are caused by dirt and oil getting trapped in pores. Herbs like turmeric can help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria, making it easier to get rid of blackheads.  Some people with oily skin may also get rosacea, a condition that causes redness and flushing on the skin. This is often caused by increased blood flow to the skin, which can be triggered by oily skin. Herbs like aloe vera can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation.  Additionally, oily skin can lead to premature aging. This is because the skin's natural moisture barrier is disrupted, allowing wrinkles and fine lines to form. Herbs like chamomile can help reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, promoting healthy skin cell growth.  Finally, people with oily skin may experience dandruff, a scalp condition that causes flakes of skin. This is often caused by an overproduction of oil on the scalp. Herbs like neem can help reduce oil production and kill fungi that can cause dandruff.
